untranslatable_reason: The Double Top pattern requires detection of two distinct peaks at approximately the same price level with a valley between them. This requires multi-bar pattern recognition (peak detection, valley identification, and measurement of peak separation ~46 days) that cannot be expressed using point-in-time indicator conditions with the allowed operators. The pattern also requires explicit prevention of a third peak forming before confirmation. These structural pattern requirements exceed the capabilities of single-bar, comparison-based rule expressions.
translation_notes: A proxy entry using Donchian Channel breakdown (close below 20-period low) captures the breakout direction (DOWN) and confirmation concept, but does not capture: (1) the two-peak structure, (2) approximate price equality of peaks, (3) ~46-day separation between peaks, (4) explicit valley identification, (5) prohibition of third peaks. Manual pattern detection or a dedicated pattern-recognition module is required for proper Double Top identification per Bulkowski's specifications.
